title: capsicum indicum var. aviculare
intro: Capsicum indicum var. aviculare, also known as 'Bird's Eye Chili', is a variety of the capsicum plant, specifically a type of small, round, and intensely red chili pepper native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties due to its high content of capsaicin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of consuming Bird's Eye Chili include reducing pain and inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of Bird's Eye Chili include making it into a paste, powder, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions and add flavor to food.
